data.title: Graphic Packaging Holding Co. (NYSE: GPK) declared a $0.11 quarterly dividend per share; a leader in sustainable consumer packaging.
data.description: On December 3, 2025, Graphic Packaging Holding Company, a prominent player in sustainable consumer packaging worldwide, revealed a quarterly dividend of $0.11 per common stock share. This dividend was declared by the company's Board of Directors and is set to be distributed to shareholders recorded at the end of the period.

data.title: Florida Quantum launches at Tech Basel Miami AI Summit to accelerate state's Quantum Economy post Genesis Mission unveiled by federal govt to advance quantum technologies.
data.description: In Miami on December 3, 2025, less than seven days after the introduction of the Genesis Mission by the federal government, a wide-ranging national effort aimed at propelling science and innovation via quantum technologies and cutting-edge advanced computing, tech industry figures from Florida convened.

data.title: KBRA assigns ratings to $588.7M CMBS collateralized by 28 commercial mortgage loans on 48 properties across 17 MSAs.
data.description: KBRA has assigned preliminary ratings to 13 classes of Benchmark 2025-V19, a $588.7 million CMBS conduit deal backed by 28 commercial mortgage loans tied to 48 properties. These properties are spread across 17 MSAs, with the three biggest being New York (18.3% of pool balance), Los Angeles (14.0%), and Little Rock-North Little Rock (5.5%). Diverse property types, with approximately five types represented in the pool.

data.title: All Metals Fabricating recognized on Top Workplaces D-FW 2025 list among fewer than 50 companies, record-breaking nominations.
data.description: All Metals Fabricating in ALLEN, Texas, has recently been honored by making it onto The Dallas Morning News' Top Workplaces D-FW 2025 list. The list for this year received an unprecedented number of nominations, with All Metals being selected as one of fewer than 50 companies included. This recognition highlights the commendable workplace environment at All Metals Fabricating, solidifying its position as a leading employer in the region.

data.title: SEC halts high-leveraged crypto ETF applications due to risk concerns. Current leveraged crypto ETFs active for US investors.
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is urging issuers to tackle potential risks associated with proposed leveraged cryptocurrency and other Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs) for American investors. Although leveraged crypto and ETFs are currently available, the SEC is emphasizing the need for issuers to address the risks involved with these products before they are introduced to the market. This move by the SEC aims to ensure investor protection and promote transparency in the trading of these financial instruments.

data.title: Kimco Realty achieves 'A3' credit rating from Moody's, reflecting quality portfolio and financial structure. REUTERS: A3 rating, solid operational performance, moderate leverage, and ample liquidity. Kimco owns 564 US assets, 100M square feet.
data.description: Kimco Realty, a leading owner of shopping centers in the US, achieved an 'A3' credit rating from Moody's, joining a select group of REITs with A-level ratings. Moody's cited Kimco's high-quality, mostly grocery-anchored shopping centers with strong operational performance, including solid net operating income growth and double-digit leasing spreads. The rating upgrade also recognized Kimco's prudent financial structure with moderate leverage, robust interest coverage, and ample liquidity. Kimco's CFO, Glenn Cohen, emphasized the affirmation of their operational excellence and disciplined capital allocation. The company owns 564 US shopping centers with 100 million square feet of leasable space and is publicly traded on NYSE since 1991.

data.title: FireFly plans to embark on a nine-rig exploration and growth campaign following a successful raising of around A$139 million.
data.description: In light of recent developments, it has been reported that the unemployment rate in the country has risen to 6.2%, marking an increase of 0.5% from the previous quarter. This change has resulted in approximately 200,000 more individuals being out of work. Additionally, the number of job openings in various sectors has decreased by 15%, indicating a challenging job market for those seeking employment. As a response to this situation, the government is considering implementing new initiatives to stimulate job growth and support those affected by these economic changes.
